Off Calendar Nov-10-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Kristi L.K. Okumoto, Esq. of FOLEY & MANSFIELD (510-590-9595), appearing for defendant / moving party Kelly Moore Paint Company, Inc.
Michael Levy, Esq.of BRENT COON & ASSOCIATES (415-489-7420), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties John H. Cardoza, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT KELLY MOORE PAINT COMPANY, INC.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Kelly Moore Paint Company, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is granted as to breach of implied warranty, fraud, civil conspiracy, enterprise liability, false representation and punitive damages and denied as to all other issues. Liberally construed, John Cardoza's deposition testimony precludes defendant from satisfying its burden of production that plaintiffs do not have and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Elizabeth Cardoza was exposed to Paco Quik-Set joint compound. Defendant satisfied its burden of production and plaintiffs did not present evidence creating a triable fact on breach of implied warranty, fraud, civil conspiracy, enterprise liability, false representation and punitive damages. Prevailing party is to prepare an order. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)
